Last edit over 5 years ago by Harpax

106
Rollo May can't really sa[y] what Socrates' daimon "is," only that it is real -- esp. for all creative men; warns of something which will take away one's a[u]to[n]o[m]y...leads one into the right relationships -- to cause on to meet certain persons (in my case eg Tessa); knows the future, is renewing, every point in detail; the inner voice which is not mere conscience, with which one can have colloquy. Is the voice of nature; is also the voice of history and historic change (!!!); it caused Socrates to become a gadfly against the state and to defy the court... [???] it, it is real, it is what took me over & which spoke to me in my physics test, etc. But what is it? Why would my daimon speak in Attic Greek same as Socrates'? Same daimon? Same voice, not his versus mine? We all have the same one and it's Attic Greek (it has a local habitation and a name?). The mystery becomes vitally wild, here. Can't be Christ; Socrates lived before Christ; likewise the Paracletos. Who, then? Whoever talked to Socrates inside his head, warned him and inspired him and guided him, it's doing it for me, too, but that doesn't tell me who or what it is "really." "You know the disease you had last year? Well, you have it again." Recognition without identification.

This is 2400 years later; it's still talking -- to me, at least. Also I guess to Goethe (of RM's book). In a way this makes sense (my voice talking attic Greek) since (sic) it's the daimon of Socrates, who was Greek; I mean, it looks like it dovetails; it's logical...but on a deeper level, although it "fits," really, the mystery deepens. "Of course it lapsed into Attic Greek. After all, that's the language it used when it talked to Socrates." As Dr. Bear said, "Why Greek, and not, say, Hebrew?" Well, here is the answer. I guess (chuckle chuckle, very nervously. This is mind-blowing. This must mean something, everything. And validated by today's foremost psychiatrist. But -- but -- Most of all, its fighting for new social forms to come, destroying the social forms which are, in Socrates' case especially, and hence regarded (he being regarded by Athens) as a bad man. Recall I was saying, "I was given the same trial as Socrates: for corrupting the youth. Told to get out of Narin County or die. I chose to leave." Ach Gott, what am I to make of this? Wo bin ich? If Rollo May were here he'd say in alarm, "Oh no, you can talk to a daimon, but not to Socrates' daimon specifically! You don't understand!" RM marvelously describes it[s] relatedness to the future, to final causes -- rather than being pushed causally by the past -- he's got it all down! Also, those creative persons inspired by it are the vanguard of society, building values for the future which will take root later. Eros is the anthesis of Thabatos: death. Eros created all life, motion and joy in the first place; before Eros emerged, "noting strirred or moved or lived" (Hesiod). Also as Angus Taylor shows, my philosophy is thorough-going Platonistic! In all my books and stories! Of Ubik and the first new revision of Plato's forms; look what Patrice said! It fits together. What does it? Who am I? So many parallels. Just how much "data" from Athens never died? Most important and significant of all: in 3 74 when I was taken over, I had a totally new worldview and it was absolutely the sum total of the highest Greek worldview, an absolutely flawless recap of all the Athenian et al philosophers did: I retrieved it utterly!!!! Now, in view of what Rollo May says, well, then is it not the spirit of Athens, then, which is Apollo Himself?

Last edit over 5 years ago by Harpax

Needs Review
129
All the Protestant had inately (sp) was his conscience. Innately, I mean. Conscience -- individual and inner, and his own, not derived. But now! Whole categories of human thought innate, inner and individual, in the same way -- conscience elaborated or added to -- multiplex systems instead of the single system of conscience, which told us only what was right, not what was so. To the Catholic, it must have seemed as impossible that a person could have an actual again bite of inwit as it would seem to the Protestant that a person could know Attic Greek innately "Conscience is a social deposit." And surely, "Knowledge of the culture and value-system of Greek times circa 400 BC is a social deposit, learned from outside entirely."

The Catholic could say, "How does this inner knowledge of right-and-wrong come into being?" and would get no answer; this isn't known. Likewise, "How does a knowledge of the Logos, of Neoplatonism, of the Eliatic Schools, come into being?" received no answer, but so it is.

It is just as obvious that conscience is a social deposit as that Attic Greek is a social deposit. It is a mystery. Perhaps it come directly from God (which the Reformers said of conscience). Now one such as I can say that of all values; not just right-and-wrong, but also, truth-versus-false, important-versus-mickeymouse. And, too, like conscience which is a quiet inner voice saying, "Such and such is wrong," this quiet inner voice speaks in Attic Greek and also gives vital information (e.g. about St. Sophia, etc.). (It's a whole audio-video school or tutorial system -- yes, tutorial; tutor to pupil, as with Aristotle and Philip).

. . . It seems to be inside things (inc. men): as the sap to the branch.

Needs Review
133
simply by dressing up and regarding not the man but the costume, as we do in a play; while the incumbent himself no longer acted of his own free will but according to his part, 'So that the scrupture might be fulfilled.' For as Thoman Mann once very well explained in a discussion of the phenomenon of 'lived myth', 'The Ego of antiquity and its consciousness of itself was different from our own, less exclusive, less sharply defined. It was, as it were, open behind; it received much from the past and by repeating it gave it presentness again.' And for such an imprecisely differentiated sense of ego, 'imitation' meant far more than we mean by the word today. I was a mythical identification...Life, or at any rate significant life, was the reconstruction of the myth in flesh and blood; it referred to and appealed to the myth; only through it, through reference to the past. Could it approve itself as genuine and significant.' And as a consequence of this solemn play of life as myth, life as quotation, time was abrogated and life became a festival, a mask: the scenic reproduction with priestly men as actors of the prototypes of the gods -- as for instance, the life and suffering of the dead and resurrected Osiris." (Joseph Campbell.)

I'm sure he selects Osiris because of the resemblance to our Passion of Christ (well, maybe he doesn't). Anyhow it is the same god really, the same myth, outside of time, and under either name capable of "abrogating time," as JC puts it. We really have all this in our mass, in which the priest, at a certain point, becomes Christ. And it is the "same" mass celebrated over and over, not a different one each time. JC of course knows this. This particular "myth", that of the death of Christ, is the only one we have, the only one which survived of all the mystery religions and other cults and religions of the antique world. We're allowed to celebrate that, but that is it. Still, it's all there. Dionysos, Zagreus, Osiris, Adonis. ("JC" is in this case Joseph Campbell, not Jesus Christ; supra.) At a certain point Christ is actually present, in the wafer & wine and also He becomes the priest, and we are once more there again; we have found our way back, a concept contained in the religion itself; viz: the dead god who returns to life. The cyclic repetition which takes place in the mass governs also the concept of why the mass is spoke and what it is about. Our god died, and was buried (gone), but then He returned. So saying, the priest therewith becomes Christ, proving the authenticity, the rightness, of the whole religion and the whole service. Proof of this worship happens each time the mass is celebrated (do I make my point? It is the basis of our religion, that after an interval our God Christ returned from the dead; i.e. came back; therefore logically it is no wonder that the "comes back" during the performance of the mass and is again present. The mass is a microcosm of the macrocosm, of the world in which this took place. The worshippers are treated to a holy miracle, of the most ultimate importance: they get to see Him return (or anyhow experience it). Once thus experienced, it cannot be doubted in the macrocosm as well -- where someday in the future He will return likewise. It is as if each time the mass (or Last Supper, "in remembrance of Me") was secretly celebrated by the early Christians, they got to unfold their miracle, about Jesus, for their own eyes alone, invisible to the (Roman-secular) world. "Thou didst not see what I saw, Robin," as Oberon puts it. I can imagine the impact in the early days of the "Fish" Christians when they gathered

135
This illustrates maybe what the Catholics say, that God (as Jesus) did not step down into this world and became a man but rather somehow drew man up to Him (to His world or realm).

It may be that because of my "transubstantiated" state in 3 74, everything I did possessed or seemed to possess the quality of ritual, of solemn enactment or even re-enactment. Everything was transfigured into archetypal modes. However, I still have the feeling that I had been waiting for that letter, a long time, perhaps virtually all of my life. The predestination was already there -- lying in my room, waiting, waiting, fearing. What I did with it was a repudiation of it. But what specifically did I repudiate? Materialism? The secular world? The Evil One? Temptation? Sin? And wasn't there something already in me allied to the letter which I threw off at the same time, with it? I changed myself. I repudiated my value system, which was life-long. The letter was a focus for that, an external embodiment that I could do something dramatic -- ritualized and enacted-- with. The real threat to me was internal, which is what I later told Mr. Kelly. The arrival of the letter only revealed to me dramatically this internal hold on me, this made-into-a-machine, or reification (not being free or alive). It, the letter arriving, told me something about myself. Which did not become true at the moment but which had always been true; I and the letter were one; whatever it was, I had been and still was, without knowing it or recognizing it (it was concealed from me). That was why it was so hard for me to deal with the letter. It related to something central to me, and linked, or tried to link, up with it. I had to impede what I conceived to be the letter reaching its internal destination. Of course, going back to the Catholic mass in which at a certain point the priest is no longer the priest, no longer a man, but the God himself, Christ -- then during the interval I was possessed or whatever it was, likewise I was no longer myself, but as I've said (supra) transubstantiation; in which case, it was not I who did what was done, not the man any longer; I was done through. What I did (the mythic or ritualistic significance) may never be known to me; for one thing, if the whole matter was a sort of "service," I can't see that outlines at all, so as to distinguish the total nature either, as well as the specific (i.e. by specific: the arrival at last of The Letter, and the repudiation of it). This is not a ritual familiar to me (as say the eucharists is).

. . . Back to Osiris: if, during the rise and mass, it could be shown to the celebrants that the dead god had indeed come back --that is, he manifested himself to them during the ritual-- then there was a basis for the celebrants regarding themselves as candidates for a return (resurrection) after their death. This would be true of Christianity, Christ being the exemplar (death & then resurrection), repeated within the experential framework of the later celebrant (outside time). Identification with the dead & reborn god would be a matter of the utmost importance, since it would assure the personal immortality. Not that Osiris lives but that I live...therefore the particular name of the god is not important, but rather the experience of his manifestation (after it is known for sure that he died). In these religions, great emphasis would be paid to his death, to insure that fact being established. Experiencing union with a dead and reborn god is much more personally significant than union with a god or a god as such, which is merely a mystic state, no more than an ASC; it may prove god exists, but we knew that anyhow; what does

142
issue. But it can't be done by men. Also, men can't rule when/if it gets done. But the job is to be done: balking, refusing to have anything to do with, any transactions with, Rome. As long as the wheat and the tares are mingled, He can't save the wheat and burn the tares. We must grow to the end, and finally separate distinctly; to so this we must turn away from the tyranny, the world state still holding power, but in an indistinct shadowy sense ("It was, it is not, but yet it is," as the Bible says).

It the Mission's Apostasy book is right ("Was, is not and yet is") then my vision of Rome in 3 74 was not a vision of a past time, and not a time dysfunction, but a vision of the essential reality around me: the true nature --normally obscured by the dokos-- of the present. This is quite different from an anamnesis of 2000 years ago, and on this point hands virtually everything; what I experienced was discernment, not anamnesis. Second, that would make the next vision, of 2 75, of spring, perhaps also a discernment. This has then nothing to do with time, or anyhow linear time, but penetration into onion-like layers of greater & greater ontological reality. This, and perhaps this alone, would explain how it could also be the discerned present US world of FLOW MY TEARS; that is surely now a time event, but one of depth into. The past, then, is with us, and yet is not (not visibly). But I will make a daring leap and say that what it showed me is that in the ultimate sense, in terms of an inexpressible kind of time (perhaps a Platonic form time, as in the way the trigrams progress, etc.) it is now really only 100 to 250 AD Rome, despite all accretional appearances; I can't explain how that can be, but it is so; a sort of very real, even ultimately real, time somehow ceased -- about the time the Mission theorizes (and the ICC likewise) that the last genuine Christians were killed and their doctrines lost or destroyed. We have bridged the gap between us and them, to reunite in continuity, so that everything is recalled, nothing is in fact lost (the ends joined, a section deleted as if it had never been: the 1500 dry spell). This is a formidable miracle, but sensible, in terms of eschatology. It is eschatologically essential. Perhaps --perhaps!-- it was a deception, by the obscure antagonist power of Rome itself, to become camouflaged and hidden from gaze; and God allows us to penetrate this disguise and see it here amongst us as always! It has taken refuge in deceit -- in the Lie. Thus, we had not merely failed to see; it was deliberately hidden by itself from us to see, the, would be to be fiercely angry in surprise. To see this would be to enter that Fish-Christian underground almost automatically. Perhaps the purpose of snipping out the 1500 years of apostasy and linking the now with the authentic fish christian period would be that the continuity of christian knowledge --which had been lost-- would be re-established, retrieved, alive again, available to us (e.g. to me, as it became, even in the original Greek). It would like living water flow from the now tangent far-past to here, being suddenly juxstaposed. Too, this could be viewed as, The results desired are the results achieved; in my case, I came to be directly linked with the Word, which flowed into me from the past amplified and explained from above. So one line of thought leads by rigid & inexorable logic to: anamnesis of circa 100/200 AD Rome (which would explain the Attic Greek words and other antique knowledge flowing to me); and another line of rigid & inexorable logic, based mostly on

143
Needs Review

(14) I talk to and am talked to by God.

Well, what more can you ask out of a transformed person? I know the future and things beyond my senses, but I'll skip that because I am not sure if that counts.

(15) I stopped drinking wine and drank beer. (16) I knew that airosol sprays were lethal; likewise cigarettes. (17) I could discern evil and could tell what was true. (18) My spelling is unchanged. (To give some continuity.) (19) I recovered from most of my quasi-physical ailments. (20) Most of my time since I spend studying theology. (21) The level of my intelligence is increased -- this includes reading retention, speed, and abstract thinking. (22) My depth perception is improved. (23) Mental operations which baffled me are now easy (i.e. mental blocks now seem gone). (24) My psychological projections are withdrawn.

The only problem is, I am in no customary sense -- maybe in no sense whatsoever-- spiritualized or exalted. In fact I seen even more mean and irratible than before. True, I do not hit anybody, but my language remains gunjy and I am crabby and domineering; my personality defects are unaltered. In the accepted sense I am not a better person. I may be healthier (maybe not that; vide the blood pressure). But I am not a good person, even though my emotions and moods are better under control. Maybe I just have a long way to go, yet.

. . . I have a sudden new thought about "Vinland" and "Portugese States of America" et al. No one is coming here to a New World, and we are not going (i.e. space flight) to a New World, but this world will be changed into a new world. These are symbols of renewal for our world, related to Spring. They are precog images of how it is going to be for us here, on what has become a depleted worn-out old world: rebirth.

260
-3-

each other in our primal guise, our authentic guise (once again?). The city was Rome & I was an early Christian. Form regression, exactly as I stated in UBIK: "The man contains —not the boy— but the former man." Likewise the city: the former city.

Regression, as I stated by way of analysis in UBIK, along the form-axis. Thus proving (to Joe Chip anyhow) Plato's theory of forms. I think in my experience of form regression, in me and around me, that I would have to agree, too. Thus anamnesis, a stripping away, a harkening back; NOT a superimposition either on the city or on me. I simply was as I really was and saw likewise, the Magdeburg belljars of Heinrich Zimmer. Then the entity which replaced me (and thought in Greek) was really me, the authentic me; and it was me thinking in Greek. The motion was from here to back there, not from back there to here. Anamnesis for sure! Another person entirely different from my contemporary ego-self was exposed. But later the accretions of time re-established themselves, which seems natural; again I saw Fullerton and again I was my present self. The palimset theory, which I worked on long ago in this thesis. To make sense out of this one must hold to Plato's theory of forms. (Of course, one could cynically explain this all away by saying that due to the sodium pentathol and seeing the golden fish necklace and hearing the words, "This was worn by the early Christians" that extreme hysterical sensi¬ tivity and suggestivy caused me to fall into a hypnoidal state, and I imagined everything...but how come I thought in correct Greek, then? I believe that the sodium pentathol and sight of the girl wearing the fish necklace were necessary causal agents, but what they did was release ^x me from the control of the contemporary object; they did not hypnotize me but rather caused me to remember; they released me, really, from bondage to current appearance. Anyhow my experience was way out of proportion to seeing one fish necklace. It might have disinhibited me, but it could hardly do a one-year job on my head. Anyhow, nothing happened until at least forty days later. TEARS was published; the Army bought it — at least a month. I knew that something absolutely dire was shaping up. Oh yes; I learned about Lorraine, and I lit the candle. One night the phosphene activity began, and soon afterward the landscape melted away & I melted away in unison with it. And the dreams of seeing great books help up open started. If the golden fish did anything it was to open the original crack, as if of a door, making me think for an instant that these were the early days of Christianity again — and that instant later became a world. By momentarily falling into that space the door was opened for me to pass through (cf the Golden Rectangle doorway I saw at the end of the experience; it led back to the Ancient World). (Which is to say by then I had returned to the modern world, and saw the ancient world, which I had been in, on the far side; and then not at all. I had made my way back, and the door disappeared, perhaps forever. What I saw beyond it was where I had been.) Well, I can hash & rehash whether he came forward or I went back, but what I am sure of (in contrast) is that both realities were present simul¬ taneously, and the Rome one was more accurate a representation. The real battle was there; the real dynamics were there. The real me was there.

from Estonia and the Xerox letter (and later on others) just after the phosphene activity — which I am surer now was due to Soviet experimentation. This news today makes me wonder even more; knowing what I know now, I deem it even more curious, even ominous. If I thought there was a connection before, I certain would tend to be convinced of it now. But this implies a tight microwave beam just to me! Or seems to. That for instance is why I wrote that letter to Fitting; to give them some feedback; likewise, I now recall, that is why I wrote Mdm. Pavolva.
